title: Subitems in App framework
excerpt:
Hi! 
Is there a way to trigger a recipe in the App framework when an subitem is created?

posts.author: jeromeskiply (Jerome Chambard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi!
Is there a way to trigger a recipe in the App framework when an subitem is created?

posts.author: AlexSavchuk (Alex Savchuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey again @jeromeskiply

That’s another good one It should be possible by using custom triggers and webhooks for the sub-item board. As you can only add recipes on the board level, you’d have to create a custom integration recipe that would watch for new sub-items and then connect the actions from there. Does that make sense?

Basically, yes, but with a bit of a twist.

-Alex

posts.author: jeromeskiply (Jerome Chambard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Alex,
Thanks for your help!
I think I understand (it is late, my brain is slow). I will try this next week.
Jerome

posts.author: AlexSavchuk (Alex Savchuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
@jeromeskiply

Awesome, I’m glad that sounds like it could work

Perhaps this awesome post by @basdebruin might help you out in your journey:

Webhook event type change_specific_column_value monday Apps & Developers
For those who are adding webhooks to a board through the API (e.g. in a subscribe event from a custom trigger) take care: lot of escaping to do! I use a function like below to inset any type of webhook to a board and returns the trigger Id. (forgive the formatting, not the best editor here static async createWebhook(token, boardId, webhookUrl, webhookType) { try { const mondayClient = initMondayClient(); mondayClient.setToken(token); const query = mutation create_webhook($…

Let me know if you’re able to get this to work. Keeping my fingers crossed

-Alex
